category_title: Students with externalizing behaviors and low frustration tolerance
minimal_context: The student is participating in a nurture group discussion where everyone is expected to take turns sharing.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's begin our group discussion. We will go around the circle and take turns sharing.

STUDENT: I'm going first! Look at what I did! Me, me, me!

TUTOR: Let's try to listen to everybody.

STUDENT: No, I don't want to listen! I'm talking now!

TUTOR: Please wait until the other pupils are finished speaking.

STUDENT: Shut up! I'm not waiting! It's all about me, me, me!

options.A: Record the behavior data in scatter plots and ABC charts when the session finishes.
options.B: Offer support and care in consistent ways and model positive social interactions.
options.C: Interview the student each term, assisting them in expanding their goal setting and discussing strategies.
options.D: Seat the pupils in a circle while the staff member remains outside the circle.
